decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells in order to deal damage quickly and efficiently. By utilizing low-cost creatures like Goblin Tomb Raider and Goblin Bushwhacker, along with powerful burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Blast, the aim is to establish board presence and control the tempo of the game. With additional cards like Experimental Synthesizer and Rally at the Hornburg for support, this deck focuses on maintaining pressure and finishing off the opponent before they can stabilize.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting opponent's plans through countering spells and removing threats, while also utilizing card draw and card filtering to maintain card advantage and find answers. The deck includes a mix of removal spells, counterspells, and evasive creatures to control the board and outvalue the opponent. Additionally, it includes a few card advantage engines like Augur of Bolas and Ninja of the Deep Hours to stay ahead in resources. The mana base is set up to support the deck's requirements with a mix of basic lands and utility lands.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly establish a strong board presence with low-cost, hexproof creatures like Gladecover Scout and Slippery Bogle, and then pump them up with Aura spells such as Ethereal Armor and Armadillo Cloak. By protecting these creatures and buffing them with auras, the deck aims to deal consistent damage to the opponent and win the game quickly. The inclusion of cards like Utopia Sprawl and Abundant Growth helps to ensure mana consistency and keep the aggression going.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently assemble the Balustrade Spy combo, which involves milling cards from the top of your library until you hit a Narcomoeba and Dread Return to then reanimate a Lotleth Giant for a devastating finish. The deck also utilizes mana dorks, card draw, and tutor effects to ensure consistency and resilience throughout the game. The goal is to combo off and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful finisher that can deal a massive amount of damage in a single turn.

The main focus of this deck is to swarm the board with cheap elf creatures and use cards like Priest of Titania and Timberwatch Elf to generate immense amounts of mana and pump up your creatures, overwhelming your opponent with an aggressive strategy. The deck also includes card draw and creature search spells to maintain its momentum and keep the pressure on the opponent. With a high number of Forests and elf creatures, this deck aims to quickly build a threatening board presence and finish the game swiftly.

The main concept of this Aggro - Affinity deck is to quickly flood the board with artifacts and use them to power up synergistic cards like Krark-Clan Shaman and Myr Enforcer, while also taking advantage of Metalcraft abilities from cards like Galvanic Blast. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and aggressive strategy, while also incorporating some control elements like Nihil Spellbomb to disrupt opponent's strategies. The mana base is streamlined to maximize efficiency and consistency in playing powerful artifact spells.

This deck focuses on aggressive plays using a combination of low-cost creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck also utilizes cards that provide advantage, like drawing cards and destroying lands, to maintain momentum. It also includes removal and disruption spells to control the opponent's board and disrupt their game plan. The deck aims to deal consistent damage while disrupting the opponent's strategy to secure a victory.

The focus of this Rakdos Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle. By using low-cost creatures like Voldaren Epicure, Sneaky Snacker, and Kitchen Imp, combined with direct damage spells such as Fiery Temper and Lightning Bolt, this deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total. Faithless Looting and Grab the Prize provide card advantage and deck thinning, while Vampire's Kiss and Alms of the Vein help to sustain the player's life total. The mana base includes both Rakdos dual lands and basic lands to ensure consistent casting of spells.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to utilize a combination of counter spells, creature removal, card draw, and faerie creatures to control the board and outvalue the opponent. By disrupting their opponent's strategy and maintaining card advantage, this deck aims to win the game through attrition and tempo advantage. With cards like Counterspell, Snuff Out, and Spellstutter Sprite, along with efficient creatures like Augur of Bolas and Ninja of the Deep Hours, the deck is designed to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent in a 1 vs 1 Pauper format.

The deck focuses on utilizing Ephemerate to continually flicker key creatures and spells in order to control the game. With God-Pharaoh's Faithful and Sunscape Familiar providing early defense, the deck aims to eventually overwhelm the opponent with card advantage and value generated from cards like Mulldrifter and Archaeomancer. By utilizing powerful counterspells like Prohibit and Snap, as well as bounce spells like Ghostly Flicker and Thraben Charm, the deck can disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win conditions through creatures and card draw.

The main goal of this deck is to quickly flood the board with artifact creatures and utilize their synergies to overwhelm the opponent. The key strategy revolves around using cheap artifacts to enable powerful spells like Galvanic Blast and Thoughtcast, while simultaneously applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Myr Enforcer and Krark-Clan Shaman. The inclusion of cards like Refurbished Familiar and Nihil Spellbomb helps to maintain card advantage and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base is optimized to support this aggressive strategy and ensure consistent casting of spells.

The main idea of this Pauper deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent using creatures like Voldaren Epicure and Sneaky Snacker, while also burning them with spells like Lightning Bolt and Fiery Temper. With cards like Guttersnipe and Kessig Flamebreather to increase the damage output, the deck aims to close out the game as fast as possible. The deck also includes removal spells like Lava Dart and Fireblast, along with card draw and mana ramp with Faithless Looting, Grab the Prize, and Highway Robbery. Overall, the deck focuses on a fast-paced aggressive strategy to win the game.

The deck focuses on quickly reducing the opponent's life total through a combination of aggressive creatures and burn spells. It aims to apply consistent pressure and deal damage efficiently with cards like Lightning Bolt, Lava Dart, and Fiery Temper. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Guttersnipe and Fireblast to maximize damage output. Faithless Looting helps with card selection and Grab the Prize and Highway Robbery provide additional resources and potential for extra damage. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy by playing low-cost creatures and using burn spells to deal damage quickly. The focus is on building a board presence early on and maintaining pressure, while also utilizing artifacts like Inventor's Axe and Experimental Synthesizer for additional damage output. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bushwhacker and Fireblast provide a strong finisher for closing out games swiftly. Overall, the goal is to win the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

This aggro red deck is focused on quickly overwhelming opponents with low-cost creatures and burn spells. With cards like Goblin Bushwhacker and Burning-Tree Emissary, the deck can flood the board early on and maintain pressure with direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Blast. Combining efficient creatures with powerful burn spells, this deck aims to win the game swiftly by attacking aggressively and finishing off opponents with a burst of direct damage. The abundance of low-cost spells allows for quick and explosive plays, putting opponents on the back foot from the start.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game by using the Urzatron lands to ramp up quickly and play powerful creatures. The deck includes a mix of creatures, artifacts, and spells to help control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It focuses on using the Urza lands in combination with cards like Expedition Map and Crop Rotation to quickly assemble Urzatron, enabling the player to cast big threats like Maelstrom Colossus and Bramble Wurm. Additionally, the deck includes some removal spells and graveyard hate to help interact with the opponent's strategy.
